preamble: The shift from the pharyngeal 'ayin' (ʕ) to the glottal fricative 'h' is a weakening process where the pharyngeal articulation shifts to a simpler glottal frication. This is often observed in the later stages of languages with complex guttural systems.

examples.note: Proto-Semitic pharyngeal sounds often merged into the glottal fricative /h/ in Ethiopian Semitic.
Dialectal weakening of 'ayin' to a simple breathy /h/.

sources: Leslau, W. (1995). Reference Grammar of Amharic. https://books.google.com/books/about/Reference_Grammar_of_Amharic.html?id=VtDX1P936FYC
